title: IMGUI Styling
use_for: organizing IMGUI styles and skins for a framework
defining semantic style tokens and spacing rules
keeping tool UI visually consistent without style sprawl
rules: prefer semantic style names over ad hoc per-widget style creation
separate spacing metrics from feature code
treat style selection as framework policy, not leaf-widget improvisation
procedure: Define semantic style roles and metrics.
Group styles by host, panel, and leaf usage.
Expose styling through explicit context or registry access.
Keep style object creation out of hot draw paths.

primary_when: the request is specifically about skins, GUIStyle organization, or theme policy in IMGUI
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about architecture, state, or event flow

solves: how to prevent style duplication and inconsistent spacing
how to expose framework-level visual rules for IMGUI tools

core_principles: Styles should communicate semantic intent, not implementation trivia.
Visual consistency should be host-managed rather than redefined per feature.
Styling decisions should not add hidden cost to every draw pass.
constraints: GUIStyle and skin creation should not happen in hot paths.
Spacing and typography choices should be centralized enough to review.
decision_rules: Use semantic categories like toolbar, inspector-section, and status-badge instead of one-off names.
Prefer style registries or context objects over free-form style construction inside leaf widgets.

avoid_patterns: new GUIStyle creation inside every draw call
feature modules hardcoding spacing and colors independently

recipes.name: Define semantic styles for an inspector-like tool
recipes.when: Use when multiple panels need consistent headers, body sections, and status rows.
steps: Create semantic roles for headers, sections, rows, and status states.
Expose them through a style context owned by the host.
Apply spacing metrics consistently across panels.

pitfalls: Ad hoc style creation scattered across features.
Visual hierarchy encoded only through magic numbers.
Theme switching that mutates style ownership unpredictably.
review_checklist: Is style creation kept out of draw hot paths?
Can panels share visual language without duplicating style code?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Performance Guidelines
references.interpretation: Use this first because style systems in IMGUI are tightly coupled to hot-path cost and allocation discipline.
references.why: Use because style creation and style access patterns directly affect hot-path cost in IMGUI.

references.title: Unity Scripting API: GUIStyle
references.interpretation: Use this to confirm the available style surface area after the domain's semantic styling rules are established.
references.why: Use for understanding style capabilities and cost implications when designing style systems.
